tasks.criterion: README is loadable by eval-rubric skill and routes eval-harness-vs-harness-build correctly [weight: 14%]: Simulate the rubric-router path in the updated `harness-kickoff/SKILL.md`: (a) Read the updated `harness-kickoff/SKILL.md`. (b) Locate the Step 1 ambiguity branch for the case where the user's project could be either `eval-harness` or `harness-build`. (c) Confirm the branch text refers the user to `plugins/trine-eval/skills/eval-rubric/rubrics/README.md` (or a relative path equivalent). (d) Read `plugins/trine-eval/skills/eval-rubric/rubrics/README.md`. (e) Confirm the README contains a section that explains the meta-vs-runtime distinction. PASSes when the evaluator can locate the ambiguity branch, the README reference is present, and the README's meta-vs-runtime explanation allows an informed choice.
README file exists at correct path [weight: 8%]: Run `test -f "plugins/trine-eval/skills/eval-rubric/rubrics/README.md"` and assert exit 0. Pre-sprint baseline: ABSENT. Post-sprint: PRESENT.

README contains the meta-vs-runtime distinction heading [weight: 8%]: Run `grep -ci 'meta.*layer\|runtime.*layer\|meta.*vs.*runtime\|eval-harness.*meta\|meta.*eval-harness\|harness-build.*runtime\|runtime.*harness-build' plugins/trine-eval/skills/eval-rubric/rubrics/README.md` and assert count >= 1. Pre-sprint baseline: 0. Post-sprint: >= 1.

README indexes all six rubric files [weight: 8%]: Run the following 6 greps and assert each returns count >= 1: `grep -c 'web-app' README.md`, `grep -c 'rag-system' README.md`, `grep -c 'cli-tool' README.md`, `grep -c 'api-service' README.md`, `grep -c 'eval-harness' README.md`, `grep -c 'harness-build' README.md`. Pre-sprint baseline: all 0 (file absent). Post-sprint: all 6 >= 1.

harness-kickoff SKILL.md contains a reference to rubrics/README.md [weight: 8%]: Run `grep -c 'rubrics/README' plugins/trine-eval/skills/harness-kickoff/SKILL.md` and assert count >= 1. Pre-sprint baseline: 0. Post-sprint: >= 1.

plugin.json description mentions both eval-harness and harness-build positioning [weight: 8%]: Run `grep -c 'eval-harness' plugins/trine-eval/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` assert >= 1 AND `grep -c 'harness-build' plugins/trine-eval/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` assert >= 1. Pre-sprint baseline: 0 for both. Post-sprint: both >= 1.

README contains a "when to pick" section for each of eval-harness and harness-build [weight: 6%]: Run `grep -ci 'when to.*pick\|pick.*when\|when to use\|use.*when\|choose.*when\|when.*choose' plugins/trine-eval/skills/eval-rubric/rubrics/README.md` and assert count >= 2. Pre-sprint baseline: file absent. Post-sprint: >= 2.

eval-rubric SKILL.md Available Rubrics list still has exactly 6 entries [weight: 4%]: Run `grep -c "^- \`" plugins/trine-eval/skills/eval-rubric/SKILL.md` and assert count == 6. Pre-sprint baseline: 6 (confirmed). Post-sprint: still 6.

README quality: meta-vs-runtime decision guide is clear, complete, and actionable [weight: 22%]: Read the full README.md. PASS requires: (a) Meta-vs-runtime distinction explicit (eval-harness = meta layer grading eval methodology; harness-build = runtime layer grading agent runtime harness per playbook). (b) Each of six rubrics has a 'when to pick this' description. (c) Index of rubric files by name with filename. (d) Disambiguation depth: a user with overlap-zone project (agent harness with eval loop) can decide which rubric applies. (e) No ambiguous/wrong guidance.

kickoff router clarification: eval-harness-vs-harness-build decision branch is legible [weight: 8%]: Read updated `harness-kickoff/SKILL.md`. PASS requires: (a) Step 1's ambiguity text extended (or nearby note) explaining eval-harness-vs-harness-build choice in overlap zone with pointer to rubrics/README.md. (b) Existing 6-entry routing table preserved verbatim. (c) JIT context scope comments remain (>= 6). (d) `eval-harness` type mentioned alongside `harness-build` in Step 1 ambiguity note.

documentation-pass quality: plugin.json and CLAUDE.md are consistent with the new positioning [weight: 6%]: Read plugin.json and CLAUDE.md. PASS requires: (a) plugin.json description accurately describes both eval-harness (meta) and harness-build (runtime) positioning, under ~200 chars, consistent with README framing. (b) CLAUDE.md three skill bullets (`/harness-kickoff`, `/harness-sprint`, `/harness-summary`) present and unchanged in content; additions permitted. (c) Any new text in CLAUDE.md consistent with README terminology.

Should NOT modify any existing rubric files (api-service.md, cli-tool.md, eval-harness.md, harness-build.md, rag-system.md, web-app.md). Verify all 6 `git diff HEAD --` commands produce empty output. Pre-sprint baseline: all empty.

Should NOT modify any prior sprint's approved markdown contract (sprint-01 through sprint-11). Verify `git diff HEAD --` for sprint-07.md through sprint-11.md all empty. Pre-sprint baseline: all empty.

Should NOT regress the 6 JIT context scope annotations in harness-kickoff SKILL.md. Verify `grep -c '<!-- Context scope at this step:' plugins/trine-eval/skills/harness-kickoff/SKILL.md` assert count >= 6. Pre-sprint baseline: 6.
tasks.verification_command: grep -c '<!-- Context scope at this step:' plugins/trine-eval/skills/harness-kickoff/SKILL.md

tasks.criterion: Should NOT remove or alter core fields in .harness/config.json (project_type, rubric, max_retries, governance.enabled). Verify `jq '.project_type == "eval-harness" and .rubric == "eval-harness" and .max_retries == 3 and .governance.enabled == true' .harness/config.json` outputs `true`. Pre-sprint baseline: true.

Should NOT remove or alter the three skill bullets in CLAUDE.md. Verify `grep -c '/harness-kickoff' CLAUDE.md`, `grep -c '/harness-sprint' CLAUDE.md`, `grep -c '/harness-summary' CLAUDE.md` each return count >= 1. Pre-sprint baseline: 1 each.

Should NOT change name or version fields in plugin.json (only description may change). Verify `jq '.name == "trine-eval" and .version == "0.2.0"' plugins/trine-eval/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` outputs `true`. Pre-sprint baseline: true.

Should NOT alter any of the 6 routing table rows in harness-kickoff SKILL.md. Verify each row individually with `grep -c '\`<project_type>\` → \`"rubric": "<rubric>"\`' SKILL.md` for all 6 rows (web-app, rag-system, cli-tool, api-service, eval-harness, harness-build). All 6 must return count >= 1. Pre-sprint baseline: 1 each.
